headline: Citi turns bullish on copper, sees price hitting $15,000 per ton within a year
summary: Investing.com -- Citi has turned bullish on copper after several months of a broadly neutral stance, raising its near-term price forecast to $14,500 per metric ton and targeting $15,000 per ton within the next six to twelve months, driven by U.S. tariff tailwinds, tighter supply and resilient demand from energy transition and AI infrastructure.

headline: Is Citigroup Stock Outperforming the Dow?
summary: While Citigroup has exceeded the Dow over the past year, Wall Street analysts maintain a cautiously optimistic outlook on the stock’s prospects.

headline: UBS Group Slashes Hundreds of Jobs in EMEA Amid CS Integration Drive
summary: UBS cuts hundreds of jobs across EMEA as the bank accelerates Credit Suisse integration and targets $13.5B in cost savings by 2026.

headline: PepsiCo (PEP) Extends Credit Facilities to Strengthen Long-Term Liquidity
summary: PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) is one of the 10 Safest Dividend Stocks to Buy Right Now. PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ:PEP) restructured its credit facilities on May 22, 2026, to maintain short- and long-term liquidity. The company replaced its existing $5 billion 364-day unsecured revolving credit facility with a new one expiring May 21, 2027. At the same […]

headline: Is Fannie Mae’s Latest Reperforming Loan Sale With Citigroup Quietly Reshaping FNMA’s Risk Profile?
summary: In late May 2026, Fannie Mae announced it had begun marketing a pool of about 2,333 reperforming loans, with bids due on June 23, 2026, working alongside Citigroup Global Markets. This move highlights how Fannie Mae actively reshapes its mortgage exposure through whole-loan sales, potentially influencing its risk profile and balance sheet mix. Next, we will examine how selling reperforming loans in partnership with Citigroup could affect Fannie Mae’s broader investment narrative. Invest in...

headline: How The Investment Story Is Shifting For Niu Technologies (NIU) After The Latest Analyst Reset
summary: Niu Technologies is back in focus after analysts reset their fair value estimate from US$4.65 to US$3.10, prompting a revised price target of US$3.50. This latest move sits alongside a Neutral rating and reflects updated assumptions that analysts see as balancing potential upside with ongoing risks. Looking ahead, you will see how this evolving analyst narrative, plus the company’s own guidance, can shape how you track the stock from here. Analyst Price Targets don't always capture the full...
